areas.name: Patient Chart And Registration
areas.summary: Core patient lookup, chart summary, demographics, contact, registration, insurance, and immunization workflows have parity coverage, with richer administrative and validation paths still open.

completed.label: Patient search and chart summary
completed.detail: React chart workspace, patient APIs, PostgreSQL seed mapping, and side-by-side patient-readiness parity are implemented.

completed.label: Patient demographic/contact mutation
completed.detail: Contact update, demographics update/restore, and temporary registration lifecycle behavior are covered.

completed.label: Insurance and immunization coverage
completed.detail: Read and mutation paths for insurance plus pediatric immunization history and entered-in-error workflows are covered.

outstanding: Patient merge, duplicate detection, richer registration validation, guarantor/subscriber detail, and patient history depth.
Portal account provisioning, patient deactivation policies, address validation catalogs, and authorization enforcement.
deferred: External vaccine registry submission, forecast rules, inventory coupling, and broader immunization registry workflows.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Broad core workflow surface, but several patient-administration details are smaller than billing, encounters, and labs.
areas.estimateRationale: Strong chart, demographics, insurance, registration, and immunization coverage; merge, portal provisioning, validation, and authorization depth remain.

areas.name: Scheduling
areas.summary: Appointment read, lifecycle, recurrence, reassignment, status, overlap, and reminder-readiness behavior is heavily covered; advanced scheduling policy remains outstanding.

completed.label: Appointment read and lifecycle
completed.detail: Future appointment search/detail plus create, cancel, delete, reschedule, arrival, check-out, no-show, category, pending status, comments, provider, facility, and billing-location edits are implemented.

completed.label: Recurring appointment behavior
completed.detail: Recurring metadata, generated series expansion, exception dates, occurrence cancel/restore/reschedule, recurring root propagation, monthly/day/workday/year units, days-of-week, monthly repeat-on, and series recurrence update are covered.

completed.label: Overlap and reminder readiness
completed.detail: Provider, patient, and room overlap tolerance plus seeded reminder due/channel/contact derivation are covered.

outstanding: Stricter provider and facility availability validation, resource conflict enforcement beyond tolerance/readiness reporting, and waitlist flows.
Reminder delivery, delivery audit, templates, and appointment-to-encounter or appointment-to-billing conversion.
deferred: Category administration beyond seeded categories and richer scheduling policy configuration.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Scheduling is functionally important and broad, but the implemented slice set already covers much of its core behavior.
areas.estimateRationale: Appointment lifecycle, recurrence, overlap, and reminder-readiness are broad; advanced availability, waitlists, and delivery/audit remain.

areas.name: Encounters And Clinical Notes
areas.summary: Encounter read/write, SOAP/vitals, sign-off, co-signature, metadata, billing/claim/procedure/diagnosis linkage, and encounter-scoped document actions are represented, with advanced clinical workflow policy still outstanding.

completed.label: Encounter lifecycle and clinical note facts
completed.detail: Encounter search/detail, vitals, SOAP create/update/delete, metadata mutation, sign-off, and co-signature readiness are covered.

completed.label: Encounter billing, claim, procedure, and diagnosis linkage
completed.detail: Read-only linkages and focused mutation paths for billing, diagnoses, fee-sheet entry, procedure-order entry, and procedure-result entry are covered.

completed.label: Encounter-scoped documents
completed.detail: Encounter attachment visibility, upload, binary upload, sign-off, denial, metadata refiling, movement, content replacement, archive/restore, lifecycle timeline, external link, revision, scanned attachment, and binary replacement are covered.

outstanding: Encounter templates, multi-form encounter packages, authorization rules, amendment policy and history depth.
Code search, coding validation, claim scrubbing, charge-capture expansion, and comprehensive audit-log export.
deferred: Direct scanner integration and full historical encounter document version chains.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Encounters combine clinical notes, documents, billing links, diagnoses, procedure links, sign-off, and policy-heavy clinical behavior.
areas.estimateRationale: Core encounter, SOAP/vitals, sign-off, document, billing, diagnosis, and procedure linkages are covered; templates and policy depth remain.

areas.name: Clinical Lists, Medications, And Prescriptions
areas.summary: Problem, allergy, medication-list, prescription, and immunization basics are parity-covered, while clinical vocabulary and prescribing depth remain future scope.

completed.label: Clinical list read model
completed.detail: Problems, allergies, medication list entries, and prescriptions render through the modernized Lists module and shared parity plan.

completed.label: Focused clinical list mutations
completed.detail: Allergy, problem-list, medication-list, prescription, and immunization lifecycle behaviors are covered.

outstanding: Vocabulary lookup, duplicate detection, diagnosis interactions, medication reconciliation, structured dose/frequency fields, and audit history.
Pharmacy routing, refill requests, controlled-substance rules, and broader e-prescribing workflow.
deferred: External formulary, pharmacy, and vaccine inventory integrations.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Clinical lists and prescribing are important, but narrower than encounters, labs, billing, documents, and production readiness in this modernization scope.
areas.estimateRationale: List and focused mutation paths are present; vocabulary lookup, medication reconciliation, pharmacy routing, and prescribing controls remain.

areas.name: Messaging And Portal-Facing Data
areas.summary: Patient message visibility, lifecycle, assignment, and content editing are covered; richer communication workflows remain open.

completed.label: Patient messages and portal-enabled state
completed.detail: Portal flag and patient-message read behavior are covered through the Messages module.

completed.label: Message lifecycle, assignment, and content
completed.detail: Create, close, soft-delete, hard-delete, reassignment, title, and body edit behavior are covered.

outstanding: Portal replies, attachments, routing queues, priority and escalation behavior, notification delivery, and audit history.
deferred: Patient portal account provisioning and external notification providers.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Messaging is a smaller domain in the current target, with most remaining depth around portal communications and notifications.
areas.estimateRationale: Message visibility and lifecycle are covered; portal replies, attachments, routing, notifications, and audit history remain substantial.

areas.name: Billing, AR, Statements, And Collections
areas.summary: Fee sheet, diagnosis/modifier/correction, claim-status, payment-posting, patient-payment, account rollups, statements, batches, and collections basics are represented; full revenue-cycle processing remains a large tail.

completed.label: Fee sheet and billing mutations
completed.detail: Read-only fee-sheet billing plus CPT creation, diagnosis coding, correction, and modifier workflows are covered.

completed.label: Claims, payments, ledger, and balances
completed.detail: Claim status, payment posting, patient payment capture, account balance, aging, ledger, and statement readiness are covered.

completed.label: Statements, batches, and collections
completed.detail: Statement generation, statement PDF export, batch candidate/package export, collections work queue, and follow-up task lifecycle are covered.

outstanding: Claim generation depth, payer adjudication, ERA/EOB import, remittance workflows, refunds/reversals beyond simple voiding, receipt printing, and statement delivery.
Audit history, charge templates, diagnosis pointer validation, modifier validation, and richer revenue-cycle exception handling.
deferred: Production clearinghouse, payer, and payment-provider integrations.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Revenue-cycle behavior has a large state space across fees, claims, payments, statements, collections, exceptions, and integrations.
areas.estimateRationale: Fee-sheet, claims status, payments, statements, batches, and collections basics are covered; full revenue-cycle processing remains large.

areas.name: Documents And Attachments
areas.summary: Patient and encounter document read, upload, retrieval, preview, sign-off, denial, metadata, archive, replacement, revision, lifecycle, scanned-readiness, and external-link behavior is broadly covered; production document operations remain open.

completed.label: Patient document read, retrieval, and mutation
completed.detail: Document metadata, text content retrieval/download, text and binary upload, sign-off, denial, metadata refiling, archive/restore, content replacement, replacement revision, binary replacement, and external-link filing are covered.

completed.label: Preview, thumbnail, lifecycle, and scanned-readiness
completed.detail: Document preview readiness, current revision facts, image inline preview, image thumbnail, PDF inline preview, lifecycle timeline, and scanned attachment readiness are covered.

outstanding: Direct scanner capture, OCR execution and queueing, generated PDF thumbnails, document routing queues, full historical version rows, role-based document workflow authorization, encryption/key management, and retention policy.
External object storage, CCDA import/export, patient-portal document access rules, and document delivery integrations.
deferred: Fax/SMS attachments and external storage provider adapters.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Document workflows span storage, previews, reviews, revisions, retention, authorization, scanner/OCR, and patient/encounter contexts.
areas.estimateRationale: Document read, upload, preview, review, archive, replacement, and revision paths are broad; scanner, OCR, storage, retention, and auth remain.

areas.name: Labs And Procedures
areas.summary: Completed results, pending orders, procedure lifecycle, corrections, specimens, clinical order queues, order transmit readiness, review queues, procedure order catalog seed/read/lifecycle/import behavior, and lab-provider catalog/directory/lifecycle/configuration/address-book linkage are parity-covered; real external lab operations remain open.

completed.label: Procedure order/result lifecycle
completed.detail: Completed result read, pending orders, create/complete/report/result/delete, encounter procedure-order/result entry, result correction, specimen metadata/detail, order correction, report correction, and report sign-off are covered.

completed.label: Review queues and lab providers
completed.detail: Clinical order queue, order transmit readiness, report review queue, patient/date/provider/lab filters, lab-provider catalog ownership, provider directory, provider lifecycle, provider transport/configuration fields, and linked address-book organization behavior are covered.

completed.label: Procedure order catalog
completed.detail: Permanent gold-data procedure order catalog rows, provider groups, orderable panel rows, modernized Reports rendering, temporary catalog item create/update/deactivate/delete lifecycle behavior, and PathGroup-style vendor compendium import semantics are covered.

outstanding: Bulk review/sign-off, reviewer assignment, queue notifications, result amendment/version history depth, chain-of-custody beyond current specimen fields, and audit-log export.
External SFTP/filesystem/web-service execution, external lab reconciliation, credential encryption/rotation policy, linked-provider deletion policy, and role-specific authorization.
deferred: Production external lab ordering interfaces and richer address-book organization lifecycle administration.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Labs and procedures include order/result lifecycle, queues, providers, catalogs, specimens, reports, and future external transport/reconciliation.
areas.estimateRationale: Procedure lifecycle, clinical order queues, order transmit readiness, review queues, providers, configuration, address-book linkage, order catalog reads/lifecycle mutations, and focused vendor compendium import are covered; external lab operations remain.

areas.name: Administration, Security, And Audit
areas.summary: Facility, user, and ACL basics are covered, but first-party authentication, policy enforcement, MFA, and audit remain major outstanding scope.

completed.label: Administration directory and lifecycle basics
completed.detail: Users/facilities directory behavior, facility lifecycle, and user lifecycle are implemented.

completed.label: Focused access-control behavior
completed.detail: Default ACL matrix, focused access-permission grant/revoke, and user group membership mutation are covered.

outstanding: First-party modernized login, ASP.NET Core Identity, password lifecycle, MFA, authorization policies, user-facility restriction matrices, broader role/permission management, and policy enforcement.
Comprehensive audit-event logging and audit export across clinical, billing, document, lab, and admin workflows.
deferred: Production identity-provider integration and security hardening beyond local parity scope.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Administration, authentication, authorization, security policy, facility restriction, and audit are cross-cutting and high-risk.
areas.estimateRationale: Directory, lifecycle, ACL, and group basics exist; login, Identity, MFA, authorization policy, facility restrictions, and audit remain major scope.

areas.name: Reports, Workbench, And Evidence
areas.summary: Operational reports, CSV export, Workbench orchestration, test management, comparison artifacts, drill-ins, and artifact links are implemented; richer evidence exploration remains future scope.

completed.label: Operational reporting
completed.detail: Gold-dataset operational reports and normalized CSV export behavior are covered.

completed.label: Workbench orchestration and evidence
completed.detail: Managed lifecycle controls, seed actions, native tests, parity plans, custom parity runs, side-by-side comparisons, drill-ins, artifact links, timeline, architecture, and progress views are implemented.

outstanding: Direct links to Playwright reports and screenshots, normalized probe detail views, accepted-difference tracking, reliability trends, and historical progress charts.
Saved report definitions and richer downloadable report formats.
deferred: Future CI integration and long-term evidence retention policy.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Workbench evidence is strategically important but smaller than the clinical, billing, document, lab, and production modernization domains.
areas.estimateRationale: Operational reporting, Workbench orchestration, comparisons, drill-ins, links, timeline, architecture, and progress views are substantial; trends and report depth remain.

areas.name: Integrations, Migration, And Production Readiness
areas.summary: The current project is strong as a local parity modernization workbench, but production migration and external integration readiness remain intentionally outside the completed slice set.

completed.label: Deterministic synthetic migration contract
completed.detail: The shared gold dataset maps to both legacy MariaDB and modernized PostgreSQL for implemented workflows.

outstanding: Legacy data extraction from real OpenEMR instances, migration rehearsal tooling, rollback strategy, production deployment, CI/CD, observability, backup/restore, and security review.
External integrations for labs, e-prescribing, vaccine registries, portal, fax/SMS, CCDA/export, clearinghouse, payment providers, and document storage.
deferred: PHI-bearing test strategy and compliance validation; this repository should continue using synthetic non-PHI data.
areas.scopeWeightRationale: Migration and production readiness are large remaining concerns, but currently bounded as later-stage scope outside the local parity loop.
areas.estimateRationale: The synthetic migration contract exists; real extraction, migration rehearsal, deployment, observability, CI/CD, backup, and external integrations remain.
